abstract: This study examines the evolving landscape of international higher education, with a specific focus on the shifting dynamics of educational policy and politics within the Romanian context. By analyzing the role of the Executive Agency for Higher Education, Research, Development and Innovation Funding (UEFISCDI), the research explores how international trends and comparative frameworks influence domestic higher education reforms. Central to this inquiry is the transition toward evidence-based policymaking and its impact on the governance of public institutions. Through a critical lens, the paper assesses how Romania navigates the pressures of the global education arena while balancing national priorities, ultimately providing insights into the complexities of modernizing higher education systems in a globalized world.
